agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier system. A user (sender or customer) requests to cancel a previously placed delivery order. Cancellation eligibility, refund handling, and operational impact all depend heavily on how far the order has progressed through the fulfillment lifecycle.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request for pickup and delivery of a package from origin to destination, with associated sender, recipient, pricing, and SLA details."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an order (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, OutForDelivery, Delivered, Cancelled, Failed)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated request to terminate an order before final delivery, subject to eligibility rules based on current order status."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The set of order statuses during which cancellation is permitted. Once an order exits this window, cancellation is no longer allowed via self-service."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A fee charged when cancellation occurs after the courier has already been dispatched or operational costs have been incurred."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the user, either full or partial, triggered by a successful cancellation."},{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery agent assigned to fulfill the order."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The act of assigning and notifying a courier to pick up an order."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled if its current status is within the cancellation window: Placed, Confirmed, or Assigned (prior to courier arrival at pickup).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing cancellation of a delivered or in-transit order would corrupt fulfillment state — the physical package has already moved. The system state must reflect physical reality.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request for an order with status PickedUp, InTransit, OutForDelivery, or Delivered MUST be rejected with an appropriate error."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A cancelled order's status must transition to Cancelled and no further status transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancelled is a terminal state. Allowing transitions out of Cancelled would corrupt the order lifecycle and could cause double-fulfillment or ghost shipments.","testable_assertion":"Once an order status is Cancelled, any attempt to transition it to another status MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only the user who placed the order (the sender) may cancel it. Recipients cannot cancel orders they did not place.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary parties to cancel orders would violate the trust contract between sender and platform and could enable denial-of-service on legitimate shipments.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a user who is not the order's sender MUST be rejected with a 403/unauthorized response."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"If a courier has already been dispatched (status = Assigned), a cancellation fee may be applied to compensate for wasted operational effort.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The cancellation fee amount and whether it applies at all is a business policy decision that can be adjusted by product/finance leadership.","testable_assertion":"When an order in Assigned status is cancelled, the system MUST calculate and record the applicable cancellation fee (which may be zero based on current policy)."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A full refund is issued for orders cancelled in Placed or Confirmed status. A partial refund (minus cancellation fee) is issued for orders cancelled in Assigned status.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund amounts and policies are business decisions subject to change based on competitive dynamics and customer satisfaction strategy.","testable_assertion":"Refund amount MUST equal full order amount for Placed/Confirmed cancellations, and order amount minus cancellation fee for Assigned cancellations."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"When a cancellation occurs on an Assigned order, the assigned courier must be notified and unassigned from the order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failing to notify and unassign the courier would cause them to attempt a pickup that no longer exists, wasting operational resources and corrupting courier availability state.","testable_assertion":"After cancellation of an Assigned order, the courier assignment record MUST be removed and a cancellation notification event MUST be emitted for the courier."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Every cancellation must record the timestamp, initiating user, order status at time of cancellation, and reason (if provided) for audit purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancellation audit trail is required for dispute resolution, financial reconciliation, and regulatory compliance. Missing records corrupt the business's ability to resolve disputes.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation record MUST be persisted with cancellationTimestamp, userId, previousStatus, and optional reason fields."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order exists in status Placed","when":"The sender requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated, and a cancellation audit record is created."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order exists in status Confirmed","when":"The sender requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated, and a cancellation audit record is created."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order exists in status Assigned (courier dispatched but has not yet picked up)","when":"The sender requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, the courier is notified and unassigned, a partial refund (minus cancellation fee) is initiated, and a cancellation audit record is created."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order exists in status PickedUp or InTransit","when":"The sender requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation request is rejected with a clear message explaining the order can no longer be cancelled because the package is already in transit."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order exists in status Delivered","when":"The sender requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation request is rejected with a message directing the user to the returns/dispute process instead."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order exists in status Cancelled","when":"The sender requests cancellation again","then":"The request is rejected as a no-op, informing the user the order is already cancelled."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A user who is NOT the sender of the order","when":"They request cancellation of that order","then":"The request is rejected with an authorization error; the order remains unchanged."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order exists in status Assigned","when":"The sender cancels and the courier notification fails to deliver","then":"The cancellation still succeeds (cancellation must not depend on courier notification success), but a retry/alert mechanism ensures the courier is eventually notified."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"Two concurrent cancellation requests arrive for the same order","when":"Both are processed","then":"Exactly one succeeds and the other receives an already-cancelled response. No double refund is issued."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order is in status Placed","when":"The sender cancels and the refund service is temporarily unavailable","then":"The order transitions to Cancelled immediately, and the refund is queued for retry. The user is informed that the refund is being processed."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent cancellation requests for the same order","expected_behavior":"Only one cancellation succeeds. The second request receives an 'already cancelled' response. Exactly one refund is issued — never a double refund. Must use optimistic locking or equivalent concurrency control on order status."},{"scenario":"Order status changes between user viewing the order and submitting cancellation (race condition)","expected_behavior":"Cancellation must validate current status at execution time, not at display time. If the order was Assigned when viewed but PickedUp by the time the request arrives, cancellation is rejected."},{"scenario":"Courier notification fails after cancellation of an Assigned order","expected_behavior":"Cancellation is NOT rolled back. The cancellation is committed, and courier notification is retried asynchronously. The system must guarantee eventual courier notification via dead-letter queue or equivalent."},{"scenario":"Refund service is unavailable at cancellation time","expected_behavior":"Order is still cancelled (status change is authoritative). Refund is queued for async processing. User is shown 'refund is being processed' rather than blocking the cancellation."},{"scenario":"User cancels an order, then the payment originally failed or is still pending","expected_behavior":"If payment was never captured, no refund is necessary — the hold is released instead. The cancellation still transitions the order to Cancelled status."},{"scenario":"Cancellation request arrives with no reason provided","expected_behavior":"Reason is optional. The cancellation proceeds, and the audit record stores reason as null/empty. The system does not force a reason to avoid friction."},{"scenario":"Order is in Assigned status and courier is already en route to pickup location","expected_behavior":"Cancellation is still permitted (courier has not yet taken possession of the package). The courier is notified to abort pickup. Cancellation fee applies per policy."},{"scenario":"Bulk cancellation — user attempts to cancel multiple orders in a single request","expected_behavior":"Each order is evaluated independently against cancellation rules. Some may succeed and others may be rejected. The response clearly indicates per-order outcome. No all-or-nothing semantics."},{"scenario":"Order was placed with a promotional discount or voucher","expected_behavior":"Refund returns the actual amount charged, not the pre-discount price. Voucher/promo reinstatement policy is a separate business decision (not automatically restored)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation reduces revenue per order but offering it builds trust and reduces chargeback/dispute costs. Cancellation fees on Assigned orders partially recover operational costs (courier dispatch, route planning). Incorrect double-refunds from race conditions would cause direct financial loss.","operational":"Cancellations on Assigned orders require real-time courier notification and re-optimization of courier routes. High cancellation rates signal upstream problems (pricing, delivery estimates, UX). Audit trail is critical for finance reconciliation and dispute handling.","user":"Self-service cancellation is a baseline user expectation. Inability to cancel creates support ticket volume, negative reviews, and churn. Clear communication about cancellation eligibility (which statuses allow it) reduces frustration. Fast refund processing builds trust.","risk":"Primary risks are double-refund from concurrency bugs, orphaned courier assignments from failed notifications, and financial leakage from missing cancellation fees. Compliance risk if audit trail is incomplete for dispute resolution.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Senders (customers placing orders)","impact":"Gain ability to self-service cancel, reducing frustration. Must understand cancellation window and fee structure clearly."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Must receive timely cancellation notifications to avoid wasted trips. Poor notification reliability directly wastes their time and erodes platform trust."},{"group":"Recipients","impact":"May be expecting a delivery that is cancelled without their input. Should receive notification that the expected delivery is no longer coming."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile refunds and cancellation fees. Require complete audit trail for each cancellation event."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Reduced inbound volume for cancellation requests. Need tooling to view cancellation history and handle edge-case escalations (e.g., out-of-window cancellation requests)."},{"group":"Operations/Dispatch","impact":"Must handle courier re-routing and availability updates when assigned orders are cancelled. Affects route optimization and courier utilization metrics."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The PM statement 'Users should be able to cancel their order' is extremely vague. It does not specify: which order statuses allow cancellation, refund policy, cancellation fee structure, whether recipients can cancel, notification requirements, or how to handle orders already in physical transit. All business rules above are inferred from standard logistics domain knowledge, NOT from an actual specification. Every rule could differ based on this company's specific policies.","high_confidence_areas":["Cancelled must be a terminal state (domain invariant in any order system)","Cancellation cannot be allowed after physical pickup (physical reality constraint)","Audit trail is required for financial reconciliation","Concurrency control is needed to prevent double refunds"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact cancellation window — which statuses are eligible","Cancellation fee amount and when it applies","Refund policy (full vs partial vs none)","Whether recipients should be notified","Whether bulk cancellation is in scope","Voucher/promo reinstatement policy"],"assumptions":["The order lifecycle follows a standard logistics flow: Placed → Confirmed → Assigned → PickedUp → InTransit → OutForDelivery → Delivered","Only the sender (not the recipient) can cancel","Cancellation is allowed up to but not including PickedUp status","A cancellation fee exists for Assigned-status cancellations","Refunds are processed asynchronously and should not block the cancellation state change","Courier notification is required but should not block cancellation"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order statuses are eligible for cancellation. The cancellation window (Placed, Confirmed, Assigned) is assumed from industry norms but could differ significantly for this business.","affected_stakeholders":["Senders","Couriers","Finance","Operations"],"options":["Allow cancellation only before courier dispatch (Placed, Confirmed)","Allow cancellation up to physical pickup (Placed, Confirmed, Assigned)","Allow cancellation at any point before delivery (including InTransit, which would require package return logistics)"],"recommendation":"Option B — allow up to Assigned status. This balances user flexibility with operational feasibility. Cancellation after physical pickup requires return logistics that is a separate feature."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define the refund and cancellation fee policy. These are critical financial decisions that affect revenue and customer satisfaction.","affected_stakeholders":["Senders","Finance","Customer Support"],"options":["Full refund always, no cancellation fee","Full refund before dispatch, partial refund after dispatch","No refund after confirmation"],"recommendation":"Full refund before dispatch, partial refund (minus a configurable cancellation fee) after dispatch. This is industry standard and balances fairness with cost recovery."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No information on whether the recipient should be notified when an order they are expecting is cancelled by the sender.","affected_stakeholders":["Recipients"],"options":["Notify recipient of cancellation","Do not notify recipient","Make it sender's choice"],"recommendation":"Notify recipient automatically. They may be waiting at the delivery location. Not notifying creates a poor experience."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Users' is undefined. Does this mean only senders, or also recipients, or also admin/support agents? Authorization model for cancellation is unspecified.","affected_stakeholders":["Senders","Recipients","Customer Support"],"options":["Only sender can cancel","Sender or recipient can cancel","Sender can self-service cancel; support agents can cancel any order with override"],"recommendation":"Sender-only self-service cancellation, with a separate admin override capability for support agents. Recipients should use a different flow (refuse delivery)."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

Cancellation eligibility, refund handling, and operational impact all depend heavily on how far the order has progressed through the fulfillment lifecycle.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request for pickup and delivery of a package from origin to destination, with
